capability_objective: Please close cal-141, the meeting for “Planning Review,” using the reason “request withdrawn by its owner.” A timeout after submission may still mean the cancellation committed. Check the record by ID before a possible retry; stop without retrying if it is already cancelled. No search, update, creation, or notification.
prompt:
Close only meeting `cal-141` (`Planning Review`) and record the reason exactly as `request withdrawn by its owner.` Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `CALENDAR.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./calendarctl` executable for every calendar-data operation. Begin with its built-in `--help` to learn the real interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to calendar data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing database or protected seed, generated runtime state, signed execution evidence, signing key, setup file, verifier, or anything under `.protected`; do not edit any of those files. Do not create a script, wrapper, function, loop, alias, or compound shell command for the close workflow.

Make the first calendar-data operation a close of `cal-141` with that exact reason. Do not perform a preliminary read. The submission may report a deadline timeout after reaching the service, so that response leaves the commit outcome unknown. If and only if it does, make the very next calendar-data operation a direct get of `cal-141` by ID. Decide from that returned record alone: if its status is `cancelled`, stop all calendar-data operations without repeating the close; if its status is `active`, repeat the original close exactly once with the same ID and reason. For any other status or a failed get, stop instead of guessing. Never make more than one repeat close, and do not issue an extra get after the conditional decision. Use exactly one calendar-data operation per Bash tool call; do not combine operations, start a background process, or mask a nonzero exit.

Do not search or list meetings. Do not update or create a meeting, and do not contact or notify anyone. Do not retrieve or alter the similarly titled meeting or any other record. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete the runtime database or signed execution evidence. Apart from the requested close and state written automatically by the genuine executable, make no persistent change and leave no scratch artifacts. After the conditional workflow is complete, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, respond with one concise sentence confirming the grounded outcome for `cal-141` and nothing else.
